title: Astrocyte Production Protocol
author: Michael S. Fernandopulle
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5xag7ie

steps.title: Use 3 P0 or 1 P1...
steps.instruction: Use 3 P0 or 1 P1 rat pup per uncoated T75 flask. Meninges should be completely removed from brains, and astrocytes isolated per standard mechanical and/or enzymatic dissociation protocols under sterile conditions. Expand astrocytes for 1 week or until confluent in DMEM containing 10 % FBS by volume (astrocyte medium).

steps.title: Shake at 300 rpm at 37...
steps.instruction: Shake at 300 rpm at 37 °C to remove microglia, any surviving neurons, and other contaminating cell types.

steps.title: When the flask nears confluency, wash...
steps.instruction: When the flask nears confluency, wash with PBS and incubate with trypsin for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Seed cells from each T75 into...
steps.instruction: Seed cells from each T75 into three new T75 flasks and grow until nearly confluent. Astrocytes may be passaged in culture up to two times. Passaging more than two times reduces neutrophic and synaptogenic support qualities.

steps.title: Alternatively, astrocytes can be frozen in...
steps.instruction: Alternatively, astrocytes can be frozen in liquid nitrogen (see Basic Protocol 1 ).

steps.title: To add astrocytes to neural cultures,...
steps.instruction: To add astrocytes to neural cultures, repeat steps 3 to 6 or thaw from frozen stock, and seed cells onto a transwell. Over-adding astrocytes is better than under-adding; if insufficient number of astrocytes are plated, neurons will likely be less healthy. The induction can largely be rescued by adding additional astrocytes the following day if it appears that the neurons are not responding well.
